findings.title: Duplicated score clamping logic could use a named helper
findings.description: The expression `Math.max(0, Math.min(100, value))` is repeated in both `getStageForScore` (line 31) and `createIntimacyState` (line 48). While this is only 2 occurrences (below the 3-use threshold where extraction is strongly recommended), a named `clampScore` helper would improve readability by encoding the intent -- that scores are constrained to [0, 100] -- rather than requiring the reader to parse the nested Math calls each time.

positive_observations: All functions are consistently short (under 20 lines) with clear single responsibilities
Named constants used throughout (TWO_HOURS_MS, COOLDOWN_MS, INTIMACY_SCORE_CHANGES) -- no magic numbers
Maximum nesting depth is 2 levels across all files -- well below thresholds
Cyclomatic complexity stays in 1-4 range for every function -- clean and testable
Pure functional core pattern applied consistently: immutable state, no side effects in domain logic
Discriminated union (TriggerResult) prevents illegal states elegantly
xstate machine definition is declarative and readable with clear state transition documentation
Guard clause pattern used effectively in evaluateTriggers (early continue for cooldown)
Nullish coalescing chain in generateResponse (line 32) is clear and reads naturally as a fallback cascade
